milestone: Post-M6 hardening — SQLite file-lock flake elimination
completionState: HARDENING_COMPLETE (no milestone status change; M6 remains SOURCE_COMPLETE)

environment.dotnetSdk: 10.0.301 (pinned by global.json, rollForward latestPatch); test runtime .NET 10.0.10
environment.supplementaryLocalChecks: macOS arm64, EnableWindowsTargeting=true Release build (0 warnings, 0 errors); test execution is Windows-CI-only for this assembly

defect.summary: On the PR #16 merge run 29997296552 (first attempt), TransferEngineTests.TemporaryUrlIsNeverPersistedInState failed with IOException: TransferState.db in use by another process when read immediately after a transfer. The identical tree passed 576/576 on runs 29995074450 and 29996973859, confirming a timing-dependent Windows file-lock flake.
defect.rootCause: SqliteTransferStateSchemaInitializer was the last state component using Microsoft.Data.Sqlite connection pooling; a pooled connection could retain the OS file handle past dispose, racing the immediate database byte read.
defect.fix: Pooling = false in SqliteTransferStateSchemaInitializer, consistent with the M4 binding store and M5 transfer store decisions. Disposal now releases the OS file handle deterministically.

validatedActions.command: GitHub Actions run 29999753949 (attempt 1): restore, Release build, dotnet test, vulnerability review, prohibited-content check, gitleaks

validatedActions.action: Full Windows CI suite, execution 2 (re-run, same commit)
Full Windows CI suite, execution 3 (re-run, same commit)
Full Windows CI suite, execution 4 (re-run, same commit)

focusedTestsAdded: SqliteSchemaFoundationTests.InitializationReleasesTheDatabaseFileHandleImmediately (exclusive open with FileShare.None immediately after initialization, no pool clearing)
stabilityProof.note: Each suite execution also runs the 25-iteration and 10-iteration stability loops, so the four runs exercised the previously flaky pattern well over one hundred times on Windows.

controlFileUpdates: PHASE_STATUS.md and HANDOFF.md record the PR #16 integration (merge commit 1c1c873cd68badc6a199c875a1e8bcb7d8cb406c, merge CI run 29997296552 passed) and the hardening scope; M7 remains NOT_STARTED and unauthorized.
unexecutedChecks: all M7 Windows Server and real-tenant acceptance checks (not authorized)
limitations: Repeated green runs plus deterministic handle-release semantics make recurrence unlikely, but no finite repetition proves a timing defect impossible; the fix removes the mechanism (pooled handle retention) rather than masking it.
